question: Can R1-R5 be uniformly and effectively derived from a strictly smaller non-vacuous substrate without hidden reintroduction?
success_conditions: the substrate has fewer independent primitive commitments than RCCD under the frozen cost preorder
all five RCCD components are derived uniformly for every system in C* satisfying P*
the derivation is effective and uses no system-specific oracle
all decoders, schedulers, histories, codebooks, proof objects, closure rules, query interfaces, and interpretation maps are charged
the substrate is non-vacuous and excludes at least one non-reasoning structure
the derivation preserves paired distinctions used in the RCCD lower bounds
failure_conditions: an RCCD component is assumed in a bridge axiom
a component is relocated into charged machinery
the substrate is equivalent to an unrestricted interpreter
the derivation is only system-by-system rather than uniform
the substrate cannot preserve a registered paired distinction
the substrate is merely a conjunction or renaming of R1-R5
decision_rule: A deeper kernel is accepted only if every success condition holds and no failure condition holds. Otherwise the candidate is rejected, classified as RCCD-equivalent compression, or unresolved.
